reports-alv-025.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Use of TOP-OF-PAGE to print recurring page headers in classical reports.

Used for:
- Repeating report title, run date, time and page number on every page
- Aligning column headers with WRITE positions in the body block
- Forcing a fresh page header with NEW-PAGE after a section break
- Distinguishing TOP-OF-PAGE from TOP-OF-LIST which fires only once

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- TOP-OF-PAGE event coded after START-OF-SELECTION block
- FORMAT COLOR 1 INTENSIFIED ON for blue header line, then FORMAT COLOR OFF
- ULINE under column headers to draw the divider
- sy-datum, sy-timlo and sy-pagno written into the title line
- WRITE column offsets in TOP-OF-PAGE must match LOOP WRITE positions in body

Interview keyword: TOP-OF-PAGE fires every page — not just the first

followupAnswerBank.question: What system variables are available in TOP-OF-PAGE?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TOP-OF-PAGE mein available key system variables: SY-PAGNO current page number rakhta hai, 1 se start hota hai aur SAP dwara automatically incremented hota hai jab ek new page start hota hai. SY-DATUM aur SY-TIMLO report runtime pe current date aur time provide karte hain. SY-UNAME identify karta hai ki kisne report run ki — printed output mein audit trail ke liye useful. SY-REPID program name deta hai. SY-LINSZ characters mein current line size deta hai, text centering ya page numbers right-align karne ke liye useful. Yeh sab professional report headers mein commonly used hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Key system variables available in TOP-OF-PAGE: SY-PAGNO holds the current page number, starting at 1 and automatically incremented by SAP when a new page starts. SY-DATUM and SY-TIMLO provide the current date and time at report runtime. SY-UNAME identifies who ran the report — useful for audit trail in printed output. SY-REPID gives the program name. SY-LINSZ gives the current line size in characters, useful for centering text or right-aligning page numbers. These are all commonly used in professional report headers.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Standard page header in every classical report: WRITE: /1 'Sales Report — XYZ Corp', 40 'Run by:', sy-uname, 70 'Date:', sy-datum. Next line: WRITE: /40 'Page:', sy-pagno, '/', lv_total_pages. Consistent, professional output across all printed pages.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Manually maintaining a page counter variable instead of using SY-PAGNO. SY-PAGNO is automatic and accurate — manual counters can get out of sync if NEW-PAGE is called conditionally or output triggers implicit page breaks.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
TOP-OF-PAGE.
  " Center the title based on line size
  DATA(lv_offset) = ( sy-linsz - 30 ) / 2.  " Center 30-char title
  WRITE AT lv_offset 'Monthly Sales Report'.

  " Right-align date and page info
  WRITE: /1 'Company: XYZ Ltd',
          45 'Run by:', sy-uname,
          70 'Date:', sy-datum.
  WRITE: /1 sy-repid,          " Program name
          60 'Time:', sy-timlo,
          70 'Page:', sy-pagno.
  ULINE.  " Separator line

  " Column headers below separator
  WRITE: /1 'Order', 12 'Date', 23 'Customer', 34 'Amount'.
  ULINE.

followupAnswerBank.question: How does NEW-PAGE interact with TOP-OF-PAGE?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: NEW-PAGE statement ek immediate page break force karta hai — current page khatam hota hai aur ek new page start hota hai. Yeh automatically new page ke liye TOP-OF-PAGE event trigger karta hai. System variable SY-PAGNO new page start hone par incremented hota hai. Yeh pattern tab use hota hai jab output ki logical sections fresh pages pe start honi chahiye — example, per customer ek page ya per cost center ek page print karna. NEW-PAGE with NO-TITLE us specific page ke liye header suppress karta hai, aur LINE-COUNT ke baad ek number new page ke liye line limit set karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: NEW-PAGE statement forces an immediate page break — the current page ends and a new page begins. This automatically triggers the TOP-OF-PAGE event for the new page. The system variable SY-PAGNO is incremented when the new page starts. This pattern is used when logical sections of output should start on fresh pages — for example, printing one page per customer or one page per cost center. NEW-PAGE with NO-TITLE suppresses the header for that specific page, and LINE-COUNT followed by a number sets the line limit for the new page.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Report requirement: 'Each company code's data should start on a new page.' Implementation: at every company code change in the output loop, call NEW-PAGE. TOP-OF-PAGE fires automatically, printing the header with the new page number. Clean, professional output with each section on its own page.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Calling NEW-PAGE inside TOP-OF-PAGE — this creates an infinite loop as NEW-PAGE triggers TOP-OF-PAGE again. NEW-PAGE must only be called from the body of the report, never from within TOP-OF-PAGE or END-OF-PAGE.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
START-OF-SELECTION.
  DATA(lv_prev_bukrs) = ''.  " Track company code breaks

  LOOP AT lt_data INTO DATA(ls_row).
    " New page for each company code group
    IF ls_row-bukrs <> lv_prev_bukrs AND lv_prev_bukrs IS NOT INITIAL.
      NEW-PAGE.  " Forces page break, triggers TOP-OF-PAGE
    ENDIF.
    lv_prev_bukrs = ls_row-bukrs.

    WRITE: / ls_row-bukrs, ls_row-belnr, ls_row-wrbtr.
  ENDLOOP.

TOP-OF-PAGE.  " Fires: (1) automatically on page 1
              "        (2) after every NEW-PAGE call
  WRITE: /1 'Financial Report — Page', sy-pagno.
  ULINE.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is TOP-OF-PAGE DURING LINE-SELECTION?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TOP-OF-PAGE DURING LINE-SELECTION interactive reports ke liye TOP-OF-PAGE event ka ek specialized variant hai. Jab user primary list mein ek line pe double-click karta hai aur ek secondary detail list open hoti hai, us secondary list mein bhi page headers hote hain. TOP-OF-PAGE primary list pages ke liye fire hota hai, aur TOP-OF-PAGE DURING LINE-SELECTION secondary aur deeper nested lists ke pages ke liye fire hota hai. System variable SY-LSIND nesting level indicate karta hai — primary list ke liye 0, first secondary list ke liye 1, aur isi tarah. Yeh different drill-down levels ke liye different headers allow karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: TOP-OF-PAGE DURING LINE-SELECTION is a specialized variant of the TOP-OF-PAGE event for interactive reports. When a user double-clicks a line in the primary list and a secondary detail list opens, that secondary list also has page headers. TOP-OF-PAGE fires for primary list pages, and TOP-OF-PAGE DURING LINE-SELECTION fires for pages in secondary and deeper nested lists. The system variable SY-LSIND indicates the nesting level — 0 for the primary list, 1 for the first secondary list, and so on. This allows different headers for different levels of drill-down.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Interactive drill-down report: primary list shows orders. User clicks order — secondary list shows line items. Secondary list has its own header with 'Order Details: 4500000123' in the page header. Implementation: TOP-OF-PAGE for primary list header, TOP-OF-PAGE DURING LINE-SELECTION for secondary list header with the selected order number in it.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Using only TOP-OF-PAGE and forgetting TOP-OF-PAGE DURING LINE-SELECTION — secondary lists have no page headers, looking unprofessional. In interactive reports, always implement both event blocks.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Primary list header
TOP-OF-PAGE.
  WRITE: /1 'Sales Order List',
          60 'Date:', sy-datum,
          70 'Page:', sy-pagno.
  WRITE: /1 'Order No.', 15 'Customer', 30 'Amount'.
  ULINE.

" Secondary list header — fires when detail list has pages
TOP-OF-PAGE DURING LINE-SELECTION.
  WRITE: /1 'Order Detail — Level', sy-lsind,  " Nesting level
          50 'Page:', sy-pagno.
  " Show which order the user drilled into
  WRITE: /1 'Selected Order:', gv_selected_vbeln.  " Global variable
  ULINE.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you print column headers in TOP-OF-PAGE aligned with output body?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TOP-OF-PAGE mein column headers ko output body ke same WRITE positions use karne chahiye alignment ensure karne ke liye. Best practice hai column positions ko constants ke roop mein ya ek header string ke roop mein define karna jo TOP-OF-PAGE aur output loop dono mein reuse ho. Dono jagah hard-coded position numbers use karna works lekin fragile hai — ek change karne ke liye doosra bhi change karna padta hai. Ek more maintainable approach hai ek header internal table ya ek header WRITE statement use karna jo output structure ko mirror kare. Header ke baad ULINE full output width matching ek visual separator provide karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Column headers in TOP-OF-PAGE must use the same WRITE positions as the output body to ensure alignment. The best practice is to define column positions as constants or as a header string that is reused in both TOP-OF-PAGE and the output loop. Using hard-coded position numbers in both places works but is fragile — changing one requires changing the other. A more maintainable approach is to use a header internal table or a header WRITE statement that mirrors the output structure. ULINE after the header provides a visual separator matching the full output width.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Code review finding: column headers misaligned with data. Root cause: body uses ls_fcat generated positions, header uses manually coded positions that were not updated when columns changed. Fix: define positions as constants — both header and body reference same constants. Alignment issue: eliminated permanently.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Writing column header positions manually without cross-checking against body WRITE positions — they get out of sync over time as columns are added or removed. Use shared constants for column positions.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Define column positions as constants — reuse in header and body
CONSTANTS: lc_col_vbeln TYPE i VALUE 1,
           lc_col_erdat TYPE i VALUE 12,
           lc_col_kunnr TYPE i VALUE 23,
           lc_col_netwr TYPE i VALUE 38.

TOP-OF-PAGE.
  WRITE: /1 'Sales Report', 60 sy-datum, 75 'Page', sy-pagno.
  " Column headers using SAME positions as body
  WRITE: AT lc_col_vbeln 'Order No.',
         AT lc_col_erdat 'Date',
         AT lc_col_kunnr 'Customer',
         AT lc_col_netwr 'Net Amount'.
  ULINE.

" Body output using same constants
LOOP AT lt_orders INTO DATA(ls_ord).
  WRITE: AT lc_col_vbeln ls_ord-vbeln,
         AT lc_col_erdat ls_ord-erdat,
         AT lc_col_kunnr ls_ord-kunnr,
         AT lc_col_netwr ls_ord-netwr.
ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: How does TOP-OF-PAGE work in ALV compared to classical reports?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Classical ABAP reports mein, TOP-OF-PAGE ek built-in event keyword hai jo WRITE-based output ke dauran har page start pe automatically fire hota hai. ALV grid reports is event ko use nahi karte kyunki ALV apna display SAP list runtime se independent manage karta hai. ALV ke liye, equivalent hai: function module ALV mein, I_CALLBACK_TOP_OF_PAGE parameter mein ek FORM routine name pass karo — wo form ALV printout ke har page ke liye called hota hai. CL_GUI_ALV_GRID wale OO ALV mein, TOP_OF_PAGE event ke liye ek handler register karo. CL_SALV_TABLE mein, get_event()->set_top_of_page_active( abap_true ) call karo aur ek handler register karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In classical ABAP reports, TOP-OF-PAGE is a built-in event keyword that fires automatically at each page start during WRITE-based output. ALV grid reports do not use this event because ALV manages its own display independent of the SAP list runtime. For ALV, the equivalent is: in function module ALV, pass a FORM routine name in the I_CALLBACK_TOP_OF_PAGE parameter — that form is called for each page of the ALV printout. In OO ALV with CL_GUI_ALV_GRID, register a handler for the TOP_OF_PAGE event. In CL_SALV_TABLE, call get_event()->set_top_of_page_active( abap_true ) and register a handler.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Migration scenario: classical report with TOP-OF-PAGE header being converted to ALV. Developer writes TOP-OF-PAGE event block — it never fires in ALV context. Fix: use I_CALLBACK_TOP_OF_PAGE = 'ALV_HEADER' parameter in REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY_LVC. Move header WRITE statements to that FORM routine. Now header prints in ALV printout too.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Writing a TOP-OF-PAGE event block in an ALV report and expecting it to fire — it never does. Always use the ALV-specific header mechanism: callback parameter for FM ALV, event handler for OO ALV.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" FM ALV: use I_CALLBACK_TOP_OF_PAGE
CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY_LVC'
  EXPORTING
    i_callback_program     = sy-repid
    i_callback_top_of_page = 'ALV_HEADER'  " FORM routine name
    it_fieldcat            = lt_fcat
  TABLES t_outtab          = lt_data.

" Header FORM routine — replaces classical TOP-OF-PAGE
FORM alv_header.
  DATA: lt_header TYPE slis_t_listheader,
        ls_header TYPE slis_listheader.
  ls_header-typ  = 'H'.          " H = heading (large bold)
  ls_header-info = 'Sales Order Report'.
  APPEND ls_header TO lt_header. CLEAR ls_header.
  ls_header-typ  = 'S'.          " S = selection info line
  ls_header-info = |Date: { sy-datum } | &
                   |Run by: { sy-uname }|.
  APPEND ls_header TO lt_header.
  CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_COMMENTARY_WRITE'
    EXPORTING it_list_commentary = lt_header.
ENDFORM.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use FORMAT COLOR in TOP-OF-PAGE?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FORMAT COLOR statement WRITE output lines pe background color apply karta hai. TOP-OF-PAGE mein, yeh report headers ko visually distinct banata hai. SAP saat color codes provide karta hai — color 1 titles ke liye blue hai, color 4 column headers ke liye, color 5 totals ke liye. INTENSIFIED ON color ko darker banata hai. FORMAT COLOR OFF default pe reset karta hai. Color tab tak saare subsequent WRITE statements pe apply hota hai jab tak off na ho. Professional reports ke liye, TOP-OF-PAGE mein report title line ke liye color 1 use karo aur column header line ke liye color 4.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: FORMAT COLOR statement applies background color to WRITE output lines. In TOP-OF-PAGE, it makes report headers visually distinct. SAP provides seven color codes — color 1 is blue for titles, color 4 for column headers, color 5 for totals. INTENSIFIED ON makes the color darker. FORMAT COLOR OFF resets to default. The color applies to all subsequent WRITE statements until turned off. For professional reports, use color 1 for the report title line and color 4 for the column header line in TOP-OF-PAGE.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Client feedback: 'Report looks plain, hard to read.' Quick improvement: FORMAT COLOR 1 INTENSIFIED ON for report title in TOP-OF-PAGE, COLOR 4 for column headers, ULINE separator. Printed output now has clear visual hierarchy — title in blue, columns in teal, data in white. Client satisfied.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Using too many colors — every line a different color — making the report look chaotic instead of professional. Limit to two or three consistent colors: title, column headers, and totals. Keep body rows in default (no color).
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
TOP-OF-PAGE.
  " Title line — blue background
  FORMAT COLOR 1 INTENSIFIED ON.
  WRITE: /1 'Monthly Sales Report — XYZ Corporation'.
  FORMAT COLOR OFF INTENSIFIED OFF.

  " Run info line — no color, standard
  WRITE: /1 'Generated:', sy-datum, sy-timlo,
          45 'User:', sy-uname,
          65 'Page:', sy-pagno.

  " Column headers — teal/blue-green background
  FORMAT COLOR 4 INTENSIFIED OFF.
  WRITE: /1 'Order No.',
          12 'Date',
          23 'Customer',
          38 'Net Value',
          55 'Currency'.
  FORMAT COLOR OFF.
  ULINE.  " Separator line

followupAnswerBank.question: What is END-OF-PAGE and how does it complement TOP-OF-PAGE?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: END-OF-PAGE TOP-OF-PAGE ka complement hai — yeh har page ke end mein fire hota hai, last output line ke baad lekin new page begin hone se pehle. Yeh page footers ke liye use hota hai: page numbers, confidentiality notices, running totals ya print approval signatures. END-OF-PAGE enable karne ke liye, report mein REPORT statement pe LINE-COUNT addition ke through ya NEW-PAGE LINE-COUNT ke through ek explicit line count set hona chahiye. Ek defined page size ke bina, END-OF-PAGE reliably fire nahi ho sakta. Yeh practice mein TOP-OF-PAGE se kam commonly used hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: END-OF-PAGE is the complement to TOP-OF-PAGE — it fires at the end of each page, after the last output line but before the new page begins. It is used for page footers: page numbers, confidentiality notices, running totals, or print approval signatures. To enable END-OF-PAGE, the report must have an explicit line count set via the LINE-COUNT addition on the REPORT statement or via NEW-PAGE LINE-COUNT. Without a defined page size, END-OF-PAGE may not fire reliably. It is less commonly used than TOP-OF-PAGE in practice.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Requirement for printed financial report: 'Each page must have a footer with Confidential and page number.' Implementation: REPORT statement with LINE-COUNT 60. END-OF-PAGE: ULINE. WRITE: /1 'Confidential — Internal Use Only', 60 'Page', sy-pagno. Result: consistent footer on every page of the printed report.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Expecting END-OF-PAGE to fire without setting a line count on the REPORT statement. Without LINE-COUNT, SAP uses default page boundaries that may not trigger END-OF-PAGE consistently. Always set LINE-COUNT when you need footer control.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
REPORT zdemo_end_of_page LINE-COUNT 50.  " 50 lines per page
" The LINE-COUNT ensures END-OF-PAGE fires at line 50

TOP-OF-PAGE.
  WRITE: /1 'Financial Report',
          60 'Date:', sy-datum,
          75 'Page', sy-pagno.
  WRITE: /1 'Order', 15 'Amount', 30 'Currency'.
  ULINE.

" Page footer — fires at bottom of each page
END-OF-PAGE.
  ULINE.
  WRITE: /1 'CONFIDENTIAL — Internal Use Only'.
  WRITE: AT sy-linsz - 10 'Page', sy-pagno.  " Right-aligned

followupAnswerBank.question: In a printed ALV report, how do you add a header using REUSE_ALV_COMMENTARY_WRITE?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Function module approach use karne wale ALV reports mein, I_CALLBACK_TOP_OF_PAGE parameter ek FORM routine pe point karta hai. Us routine ke andar, SLIS_T_LISTHEADER type ki ek internal table build karo — har entry mein ek TYP field ('H' heading ke liye, 'S' selection info ke liye, 'A' action ke liye) aur text ke liye ek INFO field. Yeh table REUSE_ALV_COMMENTARY_WRITE ko pass karo, jo header ko standard ALV style mein grid ke upar render karta hai. Yeh header screen view aur printed ALV output dono mein appear hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In ALV reports using function module approach, the I_CALLBACK_TOP_OF_PAGE parameter points to a FORM routine. Inside that routine, build an internal table of type SLIS_T_LISTHEADER — each entry has a TYP field ('H' for heading, 'S' for selection info, 'A' for action) and an INFO field for the text. Pass this table to REUSE_ALV_COMMENTARY_WRITE, which renders the header in the standard ALV style above the grid. This header appears both in the screen view and in printed ALV output.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: ALV report delivery requirement: 'When user prints the report, each page should have the report title and selection criteria at the top.' Solution: I_CALLBACK_TOP_OF_PAGE registered. FORM routine builds SLIS_T_LISTHEADER with 'H' type for title and 'S' type for date range. REUSE_ALV_COMMENTARY_WRITE renders it. Print test: professional header on every page.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Building the header SLIS_T_LISTHEADER inside the FORM but forgetting to call REUSE_ALV_COMMENTARY_WRITE — the table is built but nothing is rendered. REUSE_ALV_COMMENTARY_WRITE is the call that actually displays the header.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
FORM alv_top_of_page.
  DATA: lt_header TYPE slis_t_listheader,
        ls_header TYPE slis_listheader.

  " Heading line — large bold text
  ls_header-typ  = 'H'.
  ls_header-info = 'Monthly Sales Order Report'.
  APPEND ls_header TO lt_header. CLEAR ls_header.

  " Selection info lines
  ls_header-typ  = 'S'.
  ls_header-key  = 'Sales Org :'.
  ls_header-info = p_vkorg.
  APPEND ls_header TO lt_header. CLEAR ls_header.

  ls_header-typ  = 'S'.
  ls_header-key  = 'Period    :'.
  ls_header-info = |{ p_from } - { p_to }|.
  APPEND ls_header TO lt_header.

  " Render the header
  CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_COMMENTARY_WRITE'
    EXPORTING it_list_commentary = lt_header.
ENDFORM.

reports-alv-026.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Interactive Report ek special type ka classical ABAP report hai jisme user sirf data nahi dekhta — kuch kar bhi sakta hai. Jaise tum ek website pe table dekho aur ek row pe click karo toh uska detail page khulta hai — waise hi interactive report mein user output line pe click kare toh naya detail list open hota hai. Ye drill-down reporting hai. Iske teen extra events hote hain: AT LINE-SELECTION jab user double-click kare (ya Enter dabaye) output line pe, AT PF<nn> jab user koi specific function key dabaye (F5, F6, F7 jaise), AT USER-COMMAND jab user toolbar pe koi custom button click kare. Ye teeno events classical report ke upar extra hain — normal classical report mein sirf data print hota hai, interactive mein user actions handle hoti hain. HIDE statement ek important tool hai — ye current output line ke saath kuch data 'yaad' rakhta hai. Jab user woh line pe click kare — AT LINE-SELECTION mein wo data available hota hai. Jaise school mein sticky note lagao book ke page pe — baad mein exactly wahi page khologe sticky note dhundh ke. Practical example: Order list dikhao → user order pe double-click kare → AT LINE-SELECTION mein order number HIDE se read karo → order ke items dikhao secondary list mein. Ye basic drill-down pattern hai jo almost every interactive report mein hota hai.
reports-alv-026.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Interactive Reports ke 3 events depth mein samjho:

1. AT LINE-SELECTION:
   - Trigger: user output line pe double-click kare ya Enter dabaye.
   - sy-lilli: selected line number (primary list mein).
   - sy-lisel: selected line content as string (raw text).
   - sy-lsind: list level — 0=primary, 1=first secondary, 2=second secondary.
   - HIDE statement: output karte waqt HIDE variable_name likhte hain. AT LINE-SELECTION mein wo variable automatically fill ho jaata hai us line ke liye.
   - GET CURSOR: zyada accurate — get cursor field ya line se exact column ya field value nikalo.
   - Pattern: primary list mein order numbers WRITE karo + HIDE ls_ord-vbeln. AT LINE-SELECTION mein READ LINE sy-lilli karo ya HIDE se vbeln lo, detail fetch karo, WRITE secondary list mein.

2. AT PF<nn>:
   - Trigger: user specific function key dabaye — AT PF5, AT PF6, AT PF7 etc.
   - nn range: 02 to 23 (PF1 is Help, reserved by SAP).
   - Use case: different function keys ke liye different actions — PF5 for print, PF6 for send email.
   - Ek important point: AT PF<nn> aur AT USER-COMMAND dono handle ho sakte hain — lekin agar dono use karo toh AT USER-COMMAND preferred hai kyunki ye zyada flexible hai.

3. AT USER-COMMAND:
   - Trigger: user toolbar mein custom button click kare.
   - sy-ucomm: pressed button ka function code.
   - GUI status: SET PF-STATUS se custom toolbar buttons add karo.
   - Ek senior developer pattern: AT USER-COMMAND mein CASE sy-ucomm se handle karo — ek handler multiple buttons ke liye. AT PF<nn> se alag alag blocks likhne ki zaroorat nahi.

4. HIDE Statement Pattern:
   - WRITE: / ls_ord-vbeln, ls_ord-erdat. HIDE ls_ord-vbeln.
   - Ye statement current line number ke saath ls_ord-vbeln ka value store karta hai.
   - AT LINE-SELECTION: sy-lilli us line ka number deta hai, aur ls_ord-vbeln automatically populated hota hai.
   - Multiple values HIDE kar sakte ho per line.

5. sy-lsind (List Stack Index):
   - 0 = primary list (main report).
   - 1 = first secondary list (first drill-down).
   - 2 = second secondary list, etc.
   - Maximum nesting: typically 20 levels.
   - LEAVE TO LIST-PROCESSING ya LEAVE SCREEN se list level control karo.
reports-alv-026.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'Interactive reports in ABAP classical reporting have three additional events beyond the standard classical report events. AT LINE-SELECTION fires when a user double-clicks or presses Enter on an output line — it is used to implement drill-down behavior, where clicking on an order opens its line items. The HIDE statement is used to associate data with each output line so it is accessible in AT LINE-SELECTION via the system variable SY-LILLI for the line number. AT PF-nn fires when a user presses a specific function key — for example AT PF5 or AT PF6 — allowing different actions per function key. AT USER-COMMAND fires when a user presses a custom toolbar button, with the function code available in SY-UCOMM. Together these three events enable a full drill-down, multi-level interactive reporting experience in classical ABAP reports.'

Hinglish tip: Interview mein HIDE statement ka mention zaroor karo — ye interactive reports ka critical tool hai. SY-LILLI aur SY-LSIND bhi mention karo — ye system variables dikhate hain ki tum interactive reports deeply jaante ho.
reports-alv-026.codeExamples:
REPORT zdemo_interactive_report.

DATA: lt_orders TYPE TABLE OF vbak.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  SELECT vbeln, erdat, kunnr, netwr
    FROM vbak INTO TABLE @lt_orders UP TO 100 ROWS.

  " Primary list output
  WRITE: /1 'Order No.', 15 'Date', 26 'Customer', 37 'Amount'.
  ULINE.
  LOOP AT lt_orders INTO DATA(ls_ord).
    WRITE: /1 ls_ord-vbeln,
            15 ls_ord-erdat,
            26 ls_ord-kunnr,
            37 ls_ord-netwr.
    HIDE ls_ord-vbeln.  " Store order number for this line
  ENDLOOP.

" EVENT 1: Double-click — drill down to line items
AT LINE-SELECTION.
  " ls_ord-vbeln auto-populated by HIDE for sy-lilli line
  SELECT vbeln, posnr, matnr, kwmeng
    FROM vbap WHERE vbeln = @ls_ord-vbeln
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_items).
  " Display secondary list
  WRITE: /1 'Items for Order:', ls_ord-vbeln.
  LOOP AT lt_items INTO DATA(ls_item).
    WRITE: /1 ls_item-posnr, 10 ls_item-matnr, 30 ls_item-kwmeng.
  ENDLOOP.

" EVENT 2: Function key press
AT PF5.
  WRITE: / 'F5 pressed — Print functionality here'.

" EVENT 3: Custom toolbar button
AT USER-COMMAND.
  CASE sy-ucomm.
    WHEN 'ZEXPORT'.
      WRITE: / 'Export action triggered'.
  ENDCASE.
reports-alv-026.interviewScriptEnglish: Interactive reports in ABAP classical reporting have three additional events beyond the standard classical report events. AT LINE-SELECTION fires when a user double-clicks or presses Enter on an output line — it is used to implement drill-down behavior, where clicking on an order opens its line items. The HIDE statement is used to associate data with each output line so it is accessible in AT LINE-SELECTION via the system variable SY-LILLI for the line number. AT PF-nn fires when a user presses a specific function key — for example AT PF5 or AT PF6 — allowing different actions per function key. AT USER-COMMAND fires when a user presses a custom toolbar button, with the function code available in SY-UCOMM. Together these three events enable a full drill-down, multi-level interactive reporting experience in classical ABAP reports.
reports-alv-026.interviewScriptHinglish: ABAP classical reporting mein interactive reports ke teen additional events hote hain standard classical report events ke beyond. AT LINE-SELECTION jab user output line pe double-click karta hai ya Enter press karta hai tab fire hota hai — yeh drill-down behavior implement karne ke liye use hota hai, jahan order pe click karne se uske line items khulte hain. HIDE statement use hota hai data ko har output line ke saath associate karne ke liye taaki woh AT LINE-SELECTION mein accessible ho system variable SY-LILLI ke through line number ke liye. AT PF-nn jab user specific function key press karta hai tab fire hota hai — for example AT PF5 ya AT PF6 — har function key per different actions allow karta hai. AT USER-COMMAND jab user custom toolbar button press karta hai tab fire hota hai, function code SY-UCOMM mein available hota hai. Yeh teen events together classical ABAP reports mein full drill-down, multi-level interactive reporting experience enable karte hain.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Trying to read the selected order number from SY-LISEL (raw line text) using string parsing instead of using HIDE.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: HIDE is cleaner, type-safe, and does not break if the line format changes. Always use HIDE for data storage in interactive reports.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Not checking SY-LSIND in AT LINE-SELECTION and trying to drill down from level 2 or deeper — causes the same drill-down logic to fire incorrectly at unexpected levels.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Always check SY-LSIND for multi-level reports.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Using GET CURSOR without checking SY-SUBRC — if the cursor is not on any specific field, GET CURSOR returns blank and SY-SUBRC is non-zero.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Always check SY-SUBRC after GET CURSOR FIELD before using the value.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Having both AT PF5 and AT USER-COMMAND handle the same function code 'ZF5' — both event blocks fire, causing duplicate processing.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: If using AT USER-COMMAND as the main handler, remove the corresponding AT PF blocks.

reports-alv-026.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Interactive classical report with drill-down via AT LINE-SELECTION.

Used for:
- Building a primary list with HIDE-stored keys for selected rows
- Drilling from VBAK header to VBAP items on user double click
- Handling function keys via AT PFn and custom toolbar via AT USER-COMMAND
- Demonstrating multi-list classical output before recommending ALV equivalent

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- HIDE ls_ord-vbeln immediately after WRITE in the primary LOOP
- AT LINE-SELECTION uses ls_ord-vbeln auto-populated by HIDE for the selected line
- SELECT vbeln, posnr, matnr, kwmeng FROM VBAP for the drill-down list
- AT PF5 for a function-key event; AT USER-COMMAND with CASE sy-ucomm for toolbar
- ULINE after primary header and primary loop body

Interview keyword: HIDE plus AT LINE-SELECTION drill-down

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the HIDE statement and how does it work in interactive reports?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: HIDE statement ek variable ki value ko current output line number ke associated ek internal stack mein store karta hai. Jab ek WRITE statement ek line output karta hai, uske immediately baad HIDE variable_name rakhne se us variable ki current value us specific line se link ho jaati hai. AT LINE-SELECTION event mein, jab SY-LILLI selected line number deta hai, HIDE framework automatically us line ke liye stored variable values restore karta hai — bina kisi explicit READ TABLE ke unhe available karte hue. Yeh output lines se drill-down handlers ko context pass karne ka standard mechanism hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The HIDE statement stores a variable's value in an internal stack associated with the current output line number. When a WRITE statement outputs a line, placing HIDE variable_name immediately after it links that variable's current value to that specific line. In the AT LINE-SELECTION event, when SY-LILLI gives the selected line number, the HIDE framework automatically restores the stored variable values for that line — making them available without any explicit READ TABLE. This is the standard mechanism for passing context from output lines to drill-down handlers.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Interactive report pattern every developer must know: LOOP AT lt_orders. WRITE: / ls_ord-vbeln. HIDE ls_ord-vbeln. ENDLOOP. AT LINE-SELECTION: ls_ord-vbeln is automatically filled — use it to SELECT order items. This is the core of every classical drill-down report.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Trying to read the selected order number from SY-LISEL (raw line text) using string parsing instead of using HIDE. HIDE is cleaner, type-safe, and does not break if the line format changes. Always use HIDE for data storage in interactive reports.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Primary list with HIDE
LOOP AT lt_orders INTO DATA(ls_ord).
  WRITE: /1 ls_ord-vbeln, 15 ls_ord-kunnr, 30 ls_ord-netwr.
  HIDE: ls_ord-vbeln,   " Store order number for this line
        ls_ord-kunnr.   " Also store customer for this line
ENDLOOP.

" AT LINE-SELECTION: HIDE variables auto-restored for clicked line
AT LINE-SELECTION.
  " ls_ord-vbeln and ls_ord-kunnr are NOW populated
  " by the HIDE framework for the line sy-lilli
  SELECT vbeln, posnr, matnr FROM vbap
    WHERE vbeln = @ls_ord-vbeln  " Use HIDEd order number
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_items).
  LOOP AT lt_items INTO DATA(ls_it).
    WRITE: / ls_it-posnr, ls_it-matnr.
  ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is SY-LSIND and how does it help in multi-level interactive reports?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SY-LSIND List Stack Index hai jo interactive reports mein current nesting level track karta hai. Level 0 primary list hai, level 1 ek drill-down ke baad first secondary list hai, level 2 next level hai, aur isi tarah. AT LINE-SELECTION mein, SY-LSIND check karke har drill-down level pe different behavior implement kar sakte ho — example, level 0 pe ek order click karne se uske items dikhte hain, level 1 pe ek item click karne se uske material details dikhte hain. Yeh SY-LSIND pe ek CASE statement use karke ek single AT LINE-SELECTION block ke saath true multi-level drill-down reporting enable karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SY-LSIND is the List Stack Index that tracks the current nesting level in interactive reports. Level 0 is the primary list, level 1 is the first secondary list after a drill-down, level 2 is the next level, and so on. In AT LINE-SELECTION, checking SY-LSIND allows implementing different behavior at each drill-down level — for example, at level 0 clicking an order shows its items, at level 1 clicking an item shows its material details. This enables true multi-level drill-down reporting with a single AT LINE-SELECTION block using a CASE statement on SY-LSIND.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Three-level drill-down report: Level 0 — sales orders list. Click order → Level 1 — order line items. Click item → Level 2 — material info. AT LINE-SELECTION: CASE sy-lsind WHEN 0 — fetch items. WHEN 1 — fetch material. WHEN 2 — MESSAGE 'Maximum detail reached' TYPE 'I'. End of drill-down gracefully.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Not checking SY-LSIND in AT LINE-SELECTION and trying to drill down from level 2 or deeper — causes the same drill-down logic to fire incorrectly at unexpected levels. Always check SY-LSIND for multi-level reports.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
AT LINE-SELECTION.
  CASE sy-lsind.  " Which level was clicked
    WHEN 0.  " User clicked primary list (order)
      " HIDE populated ls_ord-vbeln for clicked line
      SELECT vbeln, posnr, matnr, kwmeng
        FROM vbap WHERE vbeln = @ls_ord-vbeln
        INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_items).
      WRITE: /1 'Items for:', ls_ord-vbeln.
      LOOP AT lt_items INTO DATA(ls_it).
        WRITE: / ls_it-posnr, ls_it-matnr.
        HIDE ls_it-matnr.  " Store for next level
      ENDLOOP.

    WHEN 1.  " User clicked item list (line item)
      " ls_it-matnr populated from HIDE at level 1
      WRITE: /1 'Material Details:', ls_it-matnr.

    WHEN OTHERS.
      MESSAGE 'No further detail available' TYPE 'I'.
  ENDCASE.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use GET CURSOR in interactive reports?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: GET CURSOR precise information provide karta hai ki jab user double-click ya Enter press karta hai tab user ka cursor kahan positioned tha. GET CURSOR LINE DATA(lv_line) line number deta hai. GET CURSOR FIELD DATA(lv_field) cursor position pe field name deta hai. GET CURSOR VALUE DATA(lv_value) field value deta hai. Yeh HIDE se more precise hai jab tumhe pata karna ho ki konsa specific column clicked tha — example, agar user specifically customer number column click karta hai, GET CURSOR FIELD batata hai 'KUNNR' clicked tha aur GET CURSOR VALUE customer number deta hai. Yeh konsa cell clicked tha uske based pe context-sensitive actions enable karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: GET CURSOR provides precise information about where the user's cursor was positioned when they double-clicked or pressed Enter. GET CURSOR LINE DATA(lv_line) gives the line number. GET CURSOR FIELD DATA(lv_field) gives the field name at the cursor position. GET CURSOR VALUE DATA(lv_value) gives the field value. This is more precise than HIDE when you need to know which specific column was clicked — for example, if the user clicks the customer number column specifically, GET CURSOR FIELD tells you 'KUNNR' was clicked and GET CURSOR VALUE gives the customer number. This enables context-sensitive actions based on which cell was clicked.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Advanced interactive report: clicking on Customer column in the order list opens customer master detail, clicking on Material column opens material master. GET CURSOR FIELD tells which column — CASE lv_field WHEN 'KUNNR' → display customer. WHEN 'MATNR' → display material. One AT LINE-SELECTION handler, multiple context-aware behaviors.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Using GET CURSOR without checking SY-SUBRC — if the cursor is not on any specific field, GET CURSOR returns blank and SY-SUBRC is non-zero. Always check SY-SUBRC after GET CURSOR FIELD before using the value.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
AT LINE-SELECTION.
  DATA: lv_field TYPE fieldname,
        lv_value TYPE char255,
        lv_line  TYPE i.

  " Get where exactly user clicked
  GET CURSOR LINE lv_line.
  GET CURSOR FIELD lv_field
              VALUE lv_value.

  " Context-sensitive action based on clicked column
  CASE lv_field.
    WHEN 'VBELN'.   " Clicked order number column
      " Show order items
      PERFORM show_order_items USING lv_value.

    WHEN 'KUNNR'.   " Clicked customer column
      " Show customer details
      PERFORM show_customer_detail USING lv_value.

    WHEN OTHERS.
      " Default: use HIDE data (line-level drill-down)
      PERFORM show_default_detail USING lv_line.
  ENDCASE.

followupAnswerBank.question: How does AT PF<nn> differ from AT USER-COMMAND?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AT PF nn ek event block hai jo ek specific function key press ke liye fire hota hai — tum har key ke liye ek separate event block likhte ho jaise AT PF5, AT PF6. AT USER-COMMAND ek generic handler hai jo function key presses aur custom toolbar button clicks sahit kisi bhi user action ke liye fire hota hai, function code SY-UCOMM mein available ke saath. Modern approach SY-UCOMM pe CASE statement ke saath AT USER-COMMAND prefer karta hai, kyunki yeh saare user actions ek jagah handle karta hai aur more maintainable hai. AT PF nn blocks AT USER-COMMAND ke saath coexist kar sakte hain, lekin agar dono same function code handle karte hain, dono event blocks fire honge — jo double processing cause kar sakta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: AT PF nn is an event block that fires for a specific function key press — you write a separate event block for each key like AT PF5, AT PF6. AT USER-COMMAND is a generic handler that fires for any user action including function key presses and custom toolbar button clicks, with the function code available in SY-UCOMM. The modern approach prefers AT USER-COMMAND with a CASE statement on SY-UCOMM, as it handles all user actions in one place and is more maintainable. AT PF nn blocks can coexist with AT USER-COMMAND, but if both handle the same function code, both event blocks fire — which can cause double processing.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Legacy code review: 8 separate AT PF blocks — AT PF5, AT PF6... AT PF12. Each handles one action. Modern refactor: remove all AT PF blocks, use single AT USER-COMMAND with CASE sy-ucomm. Same logic, one-third the code. Easier to maintain, easier to add new actions.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Having both AT PF5 and AT USER-COMMAND handle the same function code 'ZF5' — both event blocks fire, causing duplicate processing. If using AT USER-COMMAND as the main handler, remove the corresponding AT PF blocks.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" OLD approach — separate blocks per function key
" AT PF5. PERFORM print_report.
" AT PF6. PERFORM send_email.
" AT PF7. PERFORM export_data.

" MODERN approach — single AT USER-COMMAND handler
AT USER-COMMAND.
  CASE sy-ucomm.
    WHEN 'ZPRINT' OR 'PF05'.  " Custom button OR F5 key
      PERFORM print_report.
    WHEN 'ZEMAIL' OR 'PF06'.  " Custom button OR F6 key
      PERFORM send_email.
    WHEN 'ZEXPORT' OR 'PF07'. " Custom button OR F7 key
      PERFORM export_data.
    WHEN OTHERS.
      " Unknown command — ignore or log
  ENDCASE.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you set up a custom GUI status for interactive reports?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Interactive reports ke liye ek custom GUI status transaction SE41 mein create hota hai. Tum program open karte ho, 'List' type ka ek new GUI status create karte ho, aur application toolbar mein function codes add karte ho — example, Print Selected ke liye ZPRINT ya Send Email ke liye ZEMAIL. ABAP program mein, SET PF-STATUS 'STATUS_NAME' custom toolbar activate karta hai. Yeh START-OF-SELECTION ya TOP-OF-PAGE mein placed hota hai taaki yeh first page se apply ho. AT USER-COMMAND event phir CASE statement use karke function codes handle karta hai. SET PF-STATUS ke bina, default SAP list toolbar appear karta hai bina custom buttons ke.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A custom GUI status for interactive reports is created in transaction SE41. You open the program, create a new GUI status of type 'List,' and add function codes to the application toolbar — for example, ZPRINT for Print Selected or ZEMAIL for Send Email. In the ABAP program, SET PF-STATUS 'STATUS_NAME' activates the custom toolbar. This is placed in START-OF-SELECTION or TOP-OF-PAGE so it applies from the first page. The AT USER-COMMAND event then handles the function codes using a CASE statement. Without SET PF-STATUS, the default SAP list toolbar appears with no custom buttons.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Requirement: 'Add Approve and Reject buttons to the report toolbar.' Process: SE41 → program name → create GUI status 'ZAPPROVE_STATUS' → add buttons ZAPPRV (Approve) and ZRJCT (Reject) to application toolbar. ABAP: START-OF-SELECTION → SET PF-STATUS 'ZAPPROVE_STATUS'. AT USER-COMMAND: CASE sy-ucomm WHEN 'ZAPPRV' → approval logic. Client sees custom buttons working as expected.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Creating the GUI status in SE41 but forgetting SET PF-STATUS in the program — default toolbar shows, custom buttons never appear. SET PF-STATUS is the connection between the GUI status definition and the runtime display.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Step 1: Create GUI status in SE41 with function codes
" ZPRT = Print, ZEXP = Export, ZAPPRV = Approve

" Step 2: Activate in ABAP
START-OF-SELECTION.
  SET PF-STATUS 'ZREP_TOOLBAR'.  " Activate custom GUI status
  SET TITLEBAR 'ZTITLE'.         " Optional: custom title bar

  " Write report output
  LOOP AT lt_orders INTO DATA(ls_ord).
    WRITE: / ls_ord-vbeln, ls_ord-netwr.
    HIDE ls_ord-vbeln.
  ENDLOOP.

" Step 3: Handle toolbar button clicks
AT USER-COMMAND.
  CASE sy-ucomm.
    WHEN 'ZPRT'.
      PERFORM print_selected.
    WHEN 'ZEXP'.
      PERFORM export_to_excel.
    WHEN 'ZAPPRV'.
      PERFORM approve_order USING ls_ord-vbeln.
  ENDCASE.

followupAnswerBank.question: What happens when a user double-clicks in a secondary list in an interactive report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab user ek secondary list mein double-click karta hai, AT LINE-SELECTION dobara fire hota hai SY-LSIND incremented deeper nesting level reflect karte hue. Secondary list mein stored HIDE data us level ke liye available hota hai. AT LINE-SELECTION mein SY-LSIND check karke, tum har level ka drill-down separately handle karte ho. Wapis navigate karne ke liye, users F3 ya Back press karte hain, jo current list level se exit karta hai aur previous one pe automatically return karta hai. LEAVE TO LIST-PROCESSING programmatically use ho sakta hai ek specific list level pe return force karne ke liye. Maximum practical nesting usability ke liye usually teen se char levels hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a user double-clicks in a secondary list, AT LINE-SELECTION fires again with SY-LSIND incremented to reflect the deeper nesting level. The HIDE data stored in the secondary list is available for that level. By checking SY-LSIND in AT LINE-SELECTION, you handle each level's drill-down separately. To navigate back, users press F3 or Back, which exits the current list level and returns to the previous one automatically. LEAVE TO LIST-PROCESSING can be used programmatically to force a return to a specific list level. The maximum practical nesting is usually three to four levels for usability.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Three-level drill-down tested: Level 0 — orders. Click → Level 1 — items with HIDE matnr. Click item → Level 2 — material details. F3 back to items. F3 again back to orders. All navigation working. AT LINE-SELECTION with CASE sy-lsind handles each level cleanly. Test coverage: verified all three levels and back navigation before delivery.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Not handling the case when user double-clicks on a line that has no further detail — at the deepest level, AT LINE-SELECTION fires but there is nothing to show. Always add a WHEN OTHERS case with a clear message like 'No further detail available.'
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Multi-level drill-down with clean navigation
AT LINE-SELECTION.
  CASE sy-lsind.
    WHEN 0.  " Primary: order clicked → show items
      " ls_order-vbeln from HIDE
      SELECT * FROM vbap WHERE vbeln = @ls_order-vbeln
        INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_items).
      WRITE: /1 'Items for Order:', ls_order-vbeln.
      LOOP AT lt_items INTO DATA(ls_it).
        WRITE: / ls_it-posnr, ls_it-matnr.
        HIDE ls_it-matnr.  " Store for level 2
      ENDLOOP.

    WHEN 1.  " Secondary: item clicked → show material
      " ls_it-matnr from HIDE at level 1
      SELECT SINGLE * FROM mara WHERE matnr = @ls_it-matnr
        INTO @DATA(ls_mat).
      WRITE: / 'Material:', ls_mat-matnr, ls_mat-meins.

    WHEN OTHERS.  " No deeper level
      MESSAGE 'Maximum detail level reached' TYPE 'I'.
  ENDCASE.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you prevent users from drilling down into blank lines in interactive reports?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab users non-data lines pe double-click karte hain jaise headers, separators ya blank lines, AT LINE-SELECTION fire hota hai lekin un lines ke liye HIDE variables empty hote hain — meaningless processing ya potential short dumps cause karte hue. Correct defense hai AT LINE-SELECTION ke start pe HIDE variable check karna: agar yeh IS INITIAL hai, ek message display karo aur immediately RETURN karo. Additionally, tum SY-LISEL use kar sakte ho — agar selected line blank hai, processing skip karo. Kuch developers HIDE mein ek flag field use karte hain jo sirf data lines ke liye set hota hai aur header lines ke liye nahi, check ko explicit banate hue.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When users double-click on non-data lines like headers, separators, or blank lines, AT LINE-SELECTION fires but the HIDE variables for those lines are empty — leading to meaningless processing or potential short dumps. The correct defense is to check the HIDE variable at the start of AT LINE-SELECTION: if it IS INITIAL, display a message and RETURN immediately. Additionally, you can use SY-LISEL — if the selected line is blank, skip processing. Some developers use a flag field in HIDE set only for data lines and not for header lines, making the check explicit.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: UAT defect: user clicks on ULINE separator line — report crashes with 'no data found' error. Root cause: AT LINE-SELECTION fires, ls_ord-vbeln empty because ULINE has no HIDE. Fix: IF ls_ord-vbeln IS INITIAL. MESSAGE 'Please select a data line' TYPE 'I'. RETURN. ENDIF. Defect closed.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assuming users will only click on data lines. In testing, users always find creative ways to click on unexpected areas. Always add the IS INITIAL guard check at the start of AT LINE-SELECTION.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
AT LINE-SELECTION.
  " Guard: check if user clicked a data line
  " ls_ord-vbeln is populated by HIDE only for data lines
  " Header lines (WRITE /1 'Title') and ULINE have no HIDE
  IF ls_ord-vbeln IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'Please double-click on a data line to see details'
      TYPE 'I'.
    RETURN.  " Exit without processing
  ENDIF.

  " Also guard: blank line check
  IF sy-lisel IS INITIAL.
    RETURN.  " Completely blank line — nothing to process
  ENDIF.

  " Safe to proceed — valid order number available
  SELECT vbeln, posnr, matnr FROM vbap
    WHERE vbeln = @ls_ord-vbeln
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_items).
  " Display secondary list...

followupAnswerBank.question: How do interactive reports compare to ALV for drill-down functionality?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Classical interactive reports drill-down ke liye HIDE aur AT LINE-SELECTION ke saath WRITE-based output use karte hain — developer ko line format pe full control hota hai lekin UI text-based aur fixed hota hai. ALV grid primary list ke liye ek modern sortable, filterable grid provide karta hai, OO ALV mein DOUBLE_CLICK event ke through drill-down ke saath — event row index aur column name provide karta hai, HIDE aur SY-LILLI ko replace karte hue. ALV drill-down more user-friendly hai aur modern SAP UX support karta hai. Classical interactive reports legacy systems mein abhi bhi maintained hain jahan yeh original implementation thi, aur unhe samajhna support aur enhancement work ke liye essential hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Classical interactive reports use WRITE-based output with HIDE and AT LINE-SELECTION for drill-down — the developer has full control over line format but the UI is text-based and fixed. ALV grid provides a modern sortable, filterable grid for the primary list, with drill-down via the DOUBLE_CLICK event in OO ALV — the event provides the row index and column name, replacing HIDE and SY-LILLI. ALV drill-down is more user-friendly and supports the modern SAP UX. Classical interactive reports are still maintained in legacy systems where they were the original implementation, and understanding them is essential for support and enhancement work.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: New development decision: order list with drill-down. OO ALV chosen — primary list with sorting and filtering via ALV grid, DOUBLE_CLICK event handler fetches and displays item details in a popup or a second ALV container. Same drill-down result as classical, but with ALV's built-in sort, filter, export, and layout variants on the primary list.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Building new interactive drill-down reports using classical WRITE and HIDE when OO ALV DOUBLE_CLICK is available — users get an outdated text-based interface instead of a modern sortable grid. For new development, always prefer ALV with DOUBLE_CLICK event.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" OO ALV drill-down via DOUBLE_CLICK event
CLASS lcl_handler DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS: on_double_click
               FOR EVENT double_click OF cl_gui_alv_grid
               IMPORTING e_row e_column.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lcl_handler IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD on_double_click.
    " e_row-index = clicked row number
    " e_column-fieldname = clicked column name
    READ TABLE lt_orders INDEX e_row-index
      INTO DATA(ls_ord).
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      " Fetch and display item details
      PERFORM show_order_items USING ls_ord-vbeln.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

DATA: lo_handler TYPE REF TO lcl_handler.
CREATE OBJECT lo_handler.
SET HANDLER lo_handler->on_double_click FOR lo_grid.
